name: airspace-mapper
summary: **Map and analyse wireless airspace for Red Team operations with geolocation, signal strength, and vendor enrichment. Inspired by CyberArk’s WPA-scale reconnaissance strategy.**

documentation.readme: Map and analyse wireless airspace for Red Team operations with geolocation, signal strength, and vendor enrichment. Inspired by CyberArk’s WPA-scale reconnaissance strategy.
usage_sections: Features: - Parse .22000 WPA handshake files - Perform MAC OUI → vendor enrichment via CSV - Correlate APs with signal strength from .tsv logs - Optionally map GPS coordinates using .gpx waypoints or tracks - Output structured JSON and GPX for geospatial analysis - De-duplicate targets and

arguments.name: --export-gpx
arguments.description: Export waypoints to GPX format")

arguments.name: --target
arguments.description: Target host, IP, MAC, or URL the tool operates on.

arguments.name: --port
arguments.description: Target port (default: service default).

arguments.name: --interface
arguments.description: Network interface to bind to (e.g. wlan0mon, eth0).

arguments.name: --output
arguments.description: Output file path for the report or capture.

arguments.name: --verbose
arguments.description: Increase log verbosity (repeatable for higher levels).

arguments.name: --threads
arguments.description: Number of concurrent workers.

arguments.name: --timeout
arguments.description: Per-operation timeout in seconds.

arguments.name: --rate
arguments.description: Packet / request rate limit (packets/sec).

arguments.name: --wordlist
arguments.description: Path to a wordlist (users, passwords, paths).

arguments.name: --proxy
arguments.description: HTTP or SOCKS proxy URL for outbound requests.

arguments.name: --user-agent
arguments.description: HTTP User-Agent header value.

arguments.name: --cookie
arguments.description: HTTP Cookie header value.

arguments.name: --header
arguments.description: Extra HTTP header in key:value form (repeatable).

arguments.name: --insecure
arguments.description: Skip TLS certificate verification (lab use only).

arguments.name: --json
arguments.description: Emit JSON output instead of human-readable text.

arguments.name: --no-color
arguments.description: Disable ANSI color in output.

arguments.name: --quiet
arguments.description: Suppress non-error output.

arguments.name: --dry-run
arguments.description: Print what would be done without making any changes.

arguments.name: --yes
arguments.description: Skip confirmation prompts (use with care).

arguments.name: --force
arguments.description: Force the action even if a safety check would block it.

arguments.name: --no-banner
arguments.description: Suppress banner / tool-name output.

arguments.name: --capture
arguments.description: Capture mode: write packets to a pcap file.

documentation.when_to_use: Use during the 'recon' phase against surfaces [web, cloud, iots] in the Reconnaissance category. Fits when the engagement needs: **Map and analyse wireless airspace for Red Team operations with geolocation, signal strength, and vendor enrichment. Inspired by CyberArk’s WPA-scale reconnaissance strategy.**.
documentation.why_to_use: Prefer this tool based on upstream description: Map and analyse wireless airspace for Red Team operations with geolocation, signal strength, and vendor enrichment. Inspired by CyberArk’s WPA-scale reconnaissance strategy.
documentation.how_to_use: Run the primary entrypoint as shown in upstream docs, e.g. `python3 airspace_map.py handshakes.22000 signal_log.tsv gps_track.gpx --export-gpx`. Map and analyse wireless airspace for Red Team operations with geolocation, signal strength, and vendor enrichment. Inspired by CyberArk’s WPA-scale reconnaissance strategy.

trust.warning: Attribution/index entry only. Audit provenance, code, releases and licence before use.

use_cases: When you need Reconnaissance, use airspace-mapper because **Map and analyse wireless airspace for Red Team operations with geolocation, signal strength, and vendor enrichment. Inspired by CyberArk’s WPA-scale reconnais.
When the engagement is in the recon phase, use airspace-mapper as a cataloged Reconnaissance tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `python3 airspace_map.py handshakes.22000 signal_log.tsv gps_track.gpx --export-gpx` as documented upstream for airspace-mapper.
When chaining Reconnaissance tools, pair airspace-mapper with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `airspace-mapper --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
